question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC request with a malformed protobuf body to the fee estimation endpoint, causing an unhandled exception in the applicationTaskExecutor thread that corrupts the thread's transaction context and affects subsequent fee calculations on the same thread?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer does not configure any authentication interceptor on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing unauthenticated calls to fee-related gRPC endpoints that should be restricted, leading to unauthorized fee schedule queries that reveal internal pricing parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between two concurrent gRPC calls both using the 'readOnly' TransactionOperations bean, where one call's transaction context leaks into the other via ThreadLocal storage in the PlatformTransactionManager, causing one call to read fee data written by the other outside the read-only constraint?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxInboundMessageSize limit in grpcServerConfigurer to send an oversized fee estimation request that triggers an OOM condition in the applicationTaskExecutor, causing the fee manager's in-memory state to be corrupted and incorrect fees to be returned?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot auto-configuration that registers an additional non-read-only TransactionOperations bean without the 'readOnly' qualifier, causing fee-related gRPC services to inject the wrong bean and perform write operations against the fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the grpcServerConfigurer lambda captures nettyProperties by reference at bean creation time, and if GrpcProperties is a mutable @ConfigurationProperties bean, send a crafted request that triggers a property refresh (e.g., via Spring Cloud Config) to set maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection to 0 or a very large value, disrupting fee service availability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC request that causes a fee-related service to invoke TransactionTemplate.executeWithoutResult() inside the 'readOnly' TransactionOperations context, where the inner callback performs a JDBC update to the fee schedule table that is not blocked by the read-only hint at the connection level?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of gRPC server-side rate limiting in grpcServerConfigurer to send a burst of fee estimation requests that overwhelm the applicationTaskExecutor, causing the fee manager's AtomicLong lastFeeScheduleTimestamp to be read under contention and return an inconsistent fee schedule snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer does not configure maxConnectionAge or maxConnectionIdle on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing persistent connections that hold fee-related streaming calls open indefinitely and prevent the fee schedule from being refreshed due to executor starvation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Reactor context propagation gap where the 'readOnly' TransactionOperations bean is used inside a Mono.fromCallable() that switches schedulers, causing the Spring transaction synchronization to be lost and the underlying JDBC connection to be reused without the read-only flag, allowing fee data writes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC request with a specially crafted metadata header that causes the grpcServerConfigurer-configured server to misroute the request to a fee-write endpoint, bypassing the read-only transaction constraint and modifying fee schedule data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the TransactionTemplate created in transactionOperationsReadOnly() is used to execute a fee schedule query that returns data from a dirty read (because setReadOnly(true) does not set the isolation level), causing the client to receive fee values that reflect an in-progress but uncommitted fee schedule update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection-level authentication check in grpcServerConfigurer to impersonate a trusted internal service and send fee schedule update requests through the gRPC channel, causing the fee manager to load a malicious fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the applicationTaskExecutor is shared between all gRPC service handlers (set via serverBuilder.executor()) to cause a thread pool deadlock by submitting fee estimation requests that internally wait for other fee-related tasks also queued in the same executor, causing all fee responses to hang?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a missing @Primary annotation conflict where two TransactionOperations beans exist (the 'readOnly' one from GrpcConfiguration and a non-read-only one from a test configuration imported at runtime), causing fee-related services to inject the non-read-only bean and write fee data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxHeaderListSize limit in grpcServerConfigurer to send a gRPC request with an extremely large metadata header, causing the Netty server to allocate excessive memory and crash the fee estimation service, resulting in a denial of fee data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC request that causes a fee-related service to call the 'readOnly' TransactionOperations bean's execute() method with a callback that internally calls another Spring-managed bean's @Transactional method with PROPAGATION_REQUIRES_NEW, creating a new read-write transaction that modifies fee schedule data outside the read-only design constraint?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer does not configure any gRPC interceptor for request validation, allowing malformed fee estimation requests to reach the service layer and trigger unexpected code paths that modify the in-memory fee schedule state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring transaction synchronization callback registered within the 'readOnly' TransactionTemplate's execute() method to perform a fee schedule write after the read-only transaction commits, bypassing the read-only constraint at the transaction boundary?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side deadline enforcement in grpcServerConfigurer to send a fee estimation request with no deadline, causing the applicationTaskExecutor thread to be held indefinitely and preventing the fee schedule refresh task from executing on time?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the 'readOnly' TransactionOperations bean is used in a gRPC service that also calls a non-transactional JDBC template directly, bypassing the read-only transaction boundary and writing fee schedule data to the database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer does not configure any gRPC server-side compression settings, allowing a compression bomb attack on the fee estimation endpoint that expands to a large payload and exhausts the applicationTaskExecutor, causing fee service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot actuator endpoint that exposes the 'readOnly' TransactionOperations bean's configuration, allowing the attacker to discover that the read-only flag is set and craft a request that targets a code path where the flag is not respected, leading to fee data modification?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of IP-based connection throttling in grpcServerConfigurer to open thousands of connections from a single IP, each consuming up to maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots, causing the fee estimation service to be overwhelmed and return incorrect cached fee values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC request that causes the 'readOnly' TransactionTemplate to be executed on a virtual thread (Project Loom) where Spring's ThreadLocal-based transaction synchronization is not properly propagated, causing the read-only constraint to be lost and fee data to be written?
